programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Summer Diaries
programme.desc: John Craven explores Poldark country on the north Cornwall coast, and Keeley Donovan reports from the beach in Scarborough as she discovers how people need to be more sun savvy. Paul Martin is in Surrey with actor and animal rights champion Jenny Seagrove. Horse-mad since she was a youngster, Jenny has turned her passion into a mission to rescue abandoned ponies and horses(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits houses in Liverpool and Carlisle, while Dion Dublin is off to investigate a plot of land in Bury(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Return of the documentary following the work of enforcement officers, beginning by checking takeaways in Oldham to ensure the food is safe to eat, making sure everyone gets home safely in Newcastle and cracking a gold bangle scam in Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: The programme returns after a mid-series break. A pensioner recalls how a former policeman rescued him from a slurry pit, and a woman mum describes the moment her car plunged off the road into a water-filled ditch. Nick Knowles presents(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Royal Cornwall Showground in Wadebridge as Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen and Imogen clash, Emma returns to surprising news from Zara, and Jimmi tackles a debt-ridden man prepared to sacrifice his son's future to protect himself(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple with a budget of £450,000 who want to move to rural Ayrshire, and also visits 18th- century Dumfries House, home to a fine collection of furniture(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Yardley in Birmingham to try to fulfil the wish of a man who died of cancer before he could see his garden transformed(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The money-making challenge returns with Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion setting out from Morecambe to hunt for items to sell at an auction in Sheffield(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Alex Scott present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out West
programme.desc: Seb Choudhury presents a climate change special as the regional magazine returns, hearing from the Stroud founders of Extinction Rebellion during a summer of eco protest and disruption. He also follows the Weston-super-Mare curry house owner trying to ditch plastic take-away containers, while Nick Baker reveals what impact climate change is having on UK wildlife(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Whitney and Callum's wedding arrives, but with tensions high after Callum's revelation that he had a fling with Ben, Whitney struggles to put her fears aside. Callum does his best to convince Whitney that he wants to marry her, but will she believe him? Across the Square, Sonia panics when her surprise for Whitney - Bianca - is nowhere to be found(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report on whether a crisis point in education funding has been reached, with cameras following staff and pupils at North Denes Primary in Great Yarmouth through a challenging summer term. To balance the books, the headteacher has to lose almost a fifth of her staff and cut services that help families in the wider community. Boris Johnson has pledged to boost school funding - but will it be enough, and will the extra money go to the schools that need it most?(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace return with the culinary contest as 20 famous faces from the worlds of showbusiness, music and sport all hope to follow in the footsteps of last year's champion John Partridge. Zandra Rhodes, Neil Ruddock, Joey Essex, Oti Mabuse and Andy Grant are the first five of 20 contestants to take on three challenges, with one of them leaving the competition at the end(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: It's the first Saturday of the month and Kurtan tries to make a pilgrimage to Swindon to buy the new clothes of the season. However, engineering works on the bus route and a lack of alternative transport options disrupt his plans. Meanwhile, Kerry helps the vicar run the local football practice. Cotswolds-based comedy, written by and starring Daisy May Cooper and Charlie Cooper(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: An extended compilation of the most memorable news stories of 2018, with captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop, and assorted hosts and special guests from the worlds of comedy, journalism and current affairs(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: An evening of comedy at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London. Ventriloquist Nina Conti comperes the event, with a little help from her monkey, before introducing routines by stand-up stars Rob Beckett and Jimeoin(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Summer Diaries
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in the Lake District discovering how recycled nappies and other soft plastics could be the answer to fixing the growing number of potholes in the road network(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts follow the development progress of a tenement flat in Glasgow, a two-bedroom terrace in Birmingham and a house in Swindon(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: A learner driver tries to cheat his way through his driving test using a bluetooth device, while a wealthy businessman sells unlicensed medicines that he claims can cure cancer(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Helicopter medics land on a football pitch to come to the aid of a steward, while a woman recalls the story of how a lorry ploughed into stationary traffic, including her car(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp takes the bargain hunting teams to Leominster, Herefordshire, where two teams compete to buy three items that will hopefully make the greater profit at auction. Providing a helping hand to the sides are experts Paul Laidlaw and Mark Ashley. Natasha also meets Royal Worcester aficionado Henry Sandon(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Rob tries to persuade Imogen to make peace with Karen before she leaves, and Valerie tries to convince a student who is cheating in more ways than one to tell the truth(n)

programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple and their dogs to make a fresh start in the Cornish countryside on a budget of £550,000, and also gets behind the wheel of a vintage steam engine(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich come up with plans to transform a simple lawn into a cookery-themed cottage garden for a couple in Chippenham(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson travel to the Welsh border, while silver cutlery, a croquet set, and a 110-year-old bandaged teddy bear go up for auction in Burnley(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, hosted by Matt Baker and Alex Scott(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Knowing the truth, Whitney has a big decision to make. Will the couple tie the knot, or will their wedding day end in devastation? Mel worries about where Hunter is, but her mind turns elsewhere when Phil barges in and demands to know where Louise is. Keanu tracks down Ben and tries to force him to withdraw his statement, but the pair struggle to contain their anger(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: As the town is engulfed by a storm, Jac faces a devastating confrontation with her old rival Faye Byrne and the man whom they both loved. Sacha helps a grieving family that is torn over the issue of organ donation to come to a difficult decision. Patsy Kensit reprises her role as Faye for the first time since 2010(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Holliday Grainger and Callum Turner star in a six-part crime thriller. Soldier Shaun Emery has his conviction for murder overturned because of flawed evidence, and returns home to Britain a free man. However, when damning CCTV footage of him comes to light, he ends up the subject of another investigation. Newly promoted detective Rachel Carey is assigned to the case, and quickly learns the truth is not as simple as it appears(n)

programme.title: Canny Cops
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the Durham Constabulary, who must keep the peace in a county that has been heavily affected by austerity and deprivation in recent years. Officer Terry takes on a new role in the remote village of Horden, where he pays a visit to a local woman whose house frequented by some of the area's most well-known criminals. Meanwhile, a masked man steals over £3,000 in an armed robbery at a bookmakers(n)

programme.title: Reggie Yates: Extreme Russia
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Russia, discovering what life is like for young people in what has been described as the toughest place in Europe to be gay. He visits St Petersburg to attend QueerFest, a 10-day arts and culture festival thrown in support of the LGBT community, and later hears from a young lesbian who was stabbed and left for dead by homophobes(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Steph McGovern and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Summer Diaries
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in London at the Lordship Rec, one of 27,000 urban parks which are in trouble as cash-strapped councils slash budgets and some contemplate selling off land for development. Margherita Taylor reports on the north Devon village of Croyde, as campaigners do their bit to go plastic-free(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell checks out a three-bedroom semi in Alvaston, Derby, and a basement flat in Blackpool. Dion Dublin meets the developer who bought a Liverpool terrace(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers catch up with a car dealer who sold a potential death trap, and crack down on a driveway company targeting pensioners and taking advantage of them(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Two divers describe how they found a bomb from the Second World War under a pier, and a steward at a cycling race recalls the moment a support car drove into him(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Kate Bliss are on hand as two teams test their knowledge of antiques at the Lincoln Antiques Fair. Host Charlie Ross also hears about a devastating extreme weather event that destroyed nearby Louth in 1920(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Becky is faced with a terrifying life or death situation during her first official VPAS visit when she is taken hostage by an escaped prisoner looking for revenge(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin travels to the Norfolk countryside to help a couple who are looking for a change of scenery and have a £900,000 budget(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create an exotic garden inspired by tropical rainforests for a family in Ruddington, Nottingham(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion travel around Yorkshire to find items to sell at auction. Charles finds a exciting artefact in a toilet, and Christina falls for a silk gown(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Matt Baker and Mel Giedroyc(n)

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Footage of emergency services personnel assaulted in the line of duty. An arrest in Edinburgh escalates, resulting in an officer severing an artery. Meanwhile, PC Ian Bruce, who is part of a specialist team tackling vehicle crime, finds himself alone and vulnerable after members of a gang he is chasing turn on him(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the four celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. Then, the celebrities are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge at London Zoo(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Sharon Osbourne investigates her family history. Recalling her less-than-idyllic childhood and her remote relationship with her mother, Sharon delves into her maternal family history and uncovers the difficult circumstances of her mother's and grandmother's lives. Later, Sharon discovers that she had a great grandmother who was born in the United States, but soon comes to realise that her great-great-grandparent's hope of living the American Dream did not go according to plan when they were faced with the harsh reality of work in a cotton mill(n)

programme.title: Abused by the Police
programme.desc: Following the on-going high profile case of teenager Anna Chambers, who alleges she was raped by two NYPD officers while under arrest in 2017, Ellie Flynn investigates cases of sexual assault and misconduct involving on-duty police officers and women held in their custody in America(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace goes behind the scenes with Britain's biggest food retailers to discover how technology and science are being used to bring consumers their food. In the first episode, Gregg examines the ways that supermarkets are fighting to be first with the latest food fashions, and discovers how online shopping is transforming the way people purchase their food and what they put in their trolleys. He joins Tesco's Head of Plant Innovation as he invents new vegan meals that he claims will taste like meat, and also finds out how Asda are using ingenious growing techniques to make their tomatoes sweeter than strawberries(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world, as the leading sides in the NFL continue their bids to succeed Philadelphia Eagles as Super Bowl-winners(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the Boconnoc estate in the Cornish countryside, antiques experts Jonathan Pratt and Nick Hall uncover a pair of Georgian chairs and a children's pedal fire engine. Presenter Paul Martin learns about the creation of the historic south Cornish port of Charlestown, and how it led to the downfall of its creator, Charles Rashleigh(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: In St Ives, 24-year-old Tassy turns to the internet to crowd-fund a surf school to help local girls achieve their potential and increase their self-confidence(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a mattress factory in Leeds, where he discovers how steel is stretched and coiled into springs and the advantages of natural fibres like hemp and wool. Cherry Healey talks to a sleep scientist about the benefits to reaction time from taking an afternoon nap, and Ruth Goodman tries out a Middle Ages mattress made out of a straw-stuffed sack(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Emergency Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: Two British policemen are on the hunt for drug dealers in Perth and a leading Flying Doctor subjects her new British recruits to an intensive training course(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The team's search for beneficiaries to the estate of Nathan Koskovitch reveals links to a criminal past that help his heirs fill in the blanks of their family history(n)

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Shane Richie takes an aerial journey over London to explore the effect of the Blitz on his home city, retracing the routes used by the Luftwaffe and talking to survivors(n)

programme.title: Odette (1950)
programme.desc: The French widow of an English soldier offers her services to British Intelligence, and is sent undercover as a spy in Nazi-occupied France. However, her work alongside the resistance leads to her falling into the clutches of the Gestapo. Fact-based Second World War drama, with Anna Neagle, Trevor Howard, Peter Ustinov and Marius Goring(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Michael Baggott and Anita Manning assess items at the RAF Museum in Hendon, north London, while presenter Paul Martin explores the history of one of Britain's few remaining Palladian houses(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Jeremy Vine in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: Documentary following life for staff and the animals in their care at the SSPCA's wildlife rescue centre, beginning as members of the team act as mother to a litter of orphaned fox cubs and bid a fond farewell to Babe the hedgehog - their longest-staying patient(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling a set of binoculars salvaged from a U-Boat, two boxing belts awarded to a celebrated Tongan heavyweight, a vintage mechanical money box and an old wooden high chair(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell returns with the quiz show in which teams of three people use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, hoping to succeed reigning champions the Dicers. In this opening edition of the 15th series, Pink Floyd fans the Darksiders take on bridge players the Suits in the first match, with one set of clues consisting of N: Lagos, B: Mumbai, D: Pigeon Forge, and H: Los Angeles(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The seventh first-round match sees four students from Jesus College, Oxford pit their wits against a team from the University of Manchester in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Dramatised documentary examining how Hitler and the Nazi party rose to power in Germany during the 1930s, revealing the greed, manipulation and frightening underestimation of Adolf Hitler's ambition. In 1930, Germany was a liberal democracy, with elections, parliament and the rule of law, but just four years later its leader was a dictator and its government is in the hands of murderers. Leading historians and experts take viewers inside the minds of those hellbent on gaining power and those who tried to stop them(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Sheryl Lee presents a look back at the year in the guise of her character Laura Palmer from David Lynch's show Twin Peaks, while 1990 also saw The Simpsons take the world by storm and Sinead O'Connor hit the top of the charts with Nothing Compares 2 U. Plus, Nick Park made an animated breakthrough with Creature Comforts and Paul Gascoigne found Italia 90 an emotional experience(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Untouchable: The Rise and Fall of Harvey Weinstein
programme.desc: The story of how the film producer acquired and deployed his power, charting how he allegedly abused women with seeming impunity throughout his career - from his early days as a music promoter to running movie company Miramax with his brother Bob. Women who came forward to accuse Weinstein of sexual harassment and assault tell their stories in detail, including Rosanna Arquette, Erika Rosenbaum and Paz De La Huerta, while the reporters who broke the story detail his ruthless attempts to preserve power, even as scandal threatened to engulf him(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The stress begins to get to Bob as he finishes the editing of Lenny, which has considerably overrun. Gwen prepares for the first day of the rehearsal for Chicago, where she has a cast-iron contract with the producers, with approval over almost every aspect of the production. As rehearsals begin Bob experiences chest pains and visits a hospital emergency room. Gwen arranges with a Broadway-loving doctor that Bob is given his own room, and assures him that Chicago won't proceed until he is better. True-life drama, starring Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Aspiring designers take on commercial interior challenges, as they compete to win a life changing contract. The nine remaining contestants are challenged to use their interior design skills to transform hotel bedrooms at the Chocolate Boutique Hotel in Bournemouth. The hotel has a strong chocolate theme throughout, so the competitors, split into teams of three, must incorporate dark, milk or white chocolate in their designs. At the end of the challenge, one of the contestants is sent home. Presented by Fearne Cotton(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker heads to Warwickshire to marks the centenary of the death of Joseph Arch, a farm labourer who helped form the National Agricultural Labourers' Union in 1872 and later became an MP. Elsewhere, Adam Henson battles against the elements to get his harvest safely home, and John Craven, Cerys Matthews and Simon King reveal the final 12 pictures the viewers will get to vote on in this year's Countryfile photographic competition.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Yardley in Birmingham to try to fulfil the wish of a man who died of cancer before he could see his garden transformed(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The money-making challenge returns with Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion setting out from Morecambe to hunt for items to sell at an auction in Sheffield(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers investigate an explosion that left two brothers dead, while the victim of a motor insurance scam is determined not to let the fraudsters get away with it(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer has harboured a childhood dream to catch a salmon, so together with friend Paul Whitehouse, he sets off for the River Tay in Scotland full of hope and excitement. A ghillie guides them up the intimidating river in a boat and they fish using various methods to catch the elusive fish. They stay in the heart of the Highlands, where Bob cooks Paul a traditional Scottish breakfast and enjoy a swift round of golf before resuming their fishing. The duo also reflect on luck and fate and the part it has played in their lives and later assess whether the trip has lived up to their expectations(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest Super League matches, including Salford Red Devils v Warrington Wolves and London Broncos v Leeds Rhinos(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Investigating the high value estate of illustrator Jean Crow, and an illegitimate birth proves a stumbling block to finding Philip Horsley's heirs(n)

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Ricky Tomlinson takes an aerial journey over Liverpool to explore the effect the Blitz had on his home city from the perspective of those that dropped the bombs. He also hears from survivors on the ground, including his own brother, about how they coped in the city's greatest hour of need(n)

programme.title: 633 Squadron (1964)
programme.desc: British airmen embark on a daring mission to destroy a heavily fortified German rocket-fuel plant in Norway, and to ensure success the RAF pilots have to bomb an overhanging cliff to collapse it onto the building. The plan threatens to unravel, however, when a Norwegian freedom fighter plotting a land attack to coincide with the air strike is captured by the Nazis. Second World War drama, starring Cliff Robertson, George Chakiris, Donald Houston and Harry Andrews(n)

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: Norway v England (Kick-off 5.00pm). All the action from the friendly at Brann Stadion in Bergen. Norway have a rich pedigree in the women's game and will be looking for revenge after losing 3-0 to England in a World Cup quarter-final in France this summer. However, the visitors will be keen to produce another strong performance, as head coach Phil Neville continues to build a side he hopes will be capable of making home advantage count at Euro 2021(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore an ancient penny-farthing bicycle, a battered leather briefcase, an extraordinary portrait painted by mouth, and an old clocking-on clock(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A fishing boat in Salcombe goes up in flames with the skipper still aboard, while on the Isle of Sheppey, the lifeboat crew race to the aid of a man stuck in freezing mud in the middle of the night. A dog falls into the river Thames, creating an emergency for the Chiswick crew when the owner dives in to save it - and soon needs rescuing herself(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: The chefs face their final challenge in Paris, where they must prove their worth in a luxury restaurant run by the son of one of Jason Atherton's culinary heroes. As the brigade prepares to deliver the finest in French haute cuisine, expert advisors Dale Bainbridge and Andrew Beynon offer them an added incentive - the best performing member of the group will go home with a volume of all their best recipes(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Sophie Duker, Milton Jones and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: China: A New World Order
programme.desc: Six years ago, a man largely unknown in the West was given one of the most important jobs in the world. China's new President, Xi Jinping, became the leader of a rising superpower, commander of a formidable military and the head of an economy that is crucial to people's lives around the world. This documentary tells the story of his controversial six-year rule, and how he set about transforming China into a tightly controlled state where loyalty to the Communist Party appears to be valued above everything else(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Police from the UK and Uganda trace a woman who fled Britain after killing her daughter, and officers in London are on the hunt for a man wanted in Italy for committing rape and serious assault. Meanwhile in Yorkshire, police search for a criminal who used a light aircraft to smuggle cocaine(n)

programme.title: One Day That Changed My Life
programme.desc: Andrea discovers if her life-threatening cancer has returned, while Bec plans a desperate bid for funding to save her women's recovery project. Plus, deli worker Jamie determines to be crowed the best musical comedian in the UK. Narrated by Dominic Littlewood(n)

programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Emergency Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A British policeman leads an armed raid on the home of a man feared to have a gun, and a UK flying doctor fights to save a farmer's wife who is badly injured(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The death of Ivy Henshaw uncovers a rich family history involved in an important 19th century British industry. The team also investigates the case of retired haulier, Peter Kelly(n)

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Myleene Klass takes an aerial journey over Norwich to explore the effect the Blitz had on her home city from the perspective of those that dropped the bombs(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Death (1946)
programme.desc: A Second World War bomber pilot survives jumping out of his stricken plane without a parachute and falls in love with the radio operator he had first spoken to during the fateful flight. A heavenly messenger is sent to tell him he should have died and that his presence is required in the afterlife, but he remains determined to continue his life on Earth and insists on a trial to resolve the issue. Powell and Pressburger's romantic fantasy, starring David Niven, Kim Hunter, Roger Livesey and Marius Goring(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Ragley Hall, Warwickshire, antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Charlie Ross find a selection of antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Christina gets to grips with a weighty jardiniere, Charlie tests out a horse-measuring stick on its owner and presenter Paul Martin steps into the world of folk art(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Quiz show, presented by Jeremy Vine in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: Staff at the SSPCA's wildlife rescue centre take in one of the first badger cubs of the year, a pair of otters are released, and the first brood of housemartins is set free(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests with interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team work together to restore a clapping monkey toy, an ornate Victorian travelling treasure box, and an iconic 1950s Wurlitzer jukebox(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The contestants are challenged to design a small studio in a student halls of residence in Leicester, which must allow room for sleeping, studying and socialising. With just two days to complete their makeovers, the competitors need to make the best use of their time to ensure they get their studios finished. Presented by Fearne Cotton(n)

programme.title: The Big Hospital Experiment
programme.desc: In a radical social experiment based on a scheme in Germany, 14 young volunteers between the ages of 18 and 24 spend four weeks delivering patient care on the NHS frontline at Royal Derby Hospital, aiming to find out whether volunteers could help relieve the pressures of staff on the wards and make a positive difference to patient care in the NHS(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam is taken aback when her ex-husband's father arrives in Los Angeles with the London Philharmonic and asks her for a favour. Elsewhere, Sam is invited to make a speech at the bar mitzvah of her friend's son. Comedy-drama, created by and starring Pamela Adlon(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the historic city of Cadiz in southern Spain, where he enjoys traditional tapas dishes including chickpea stews, cured pork lardons and freshly grilled mackerel(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine historical murder cases in which those convicted went to the gallows pleading their innocence. In the first edition, they investigate the case of Charlotte Bryant, who was arrested and hanged for the murder by arsenic poisoning of her husband Frederick Bryant in 1935(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom's Year with the Birds
programme.desc: To mark his death earlier this month, another chance to see a programme in which the wildlife expert sets out to film the birds near his home on the edge of Exmoor across the seasons, beginning in autumn as he prepares his new remote cameras for the winter. While he manages to locate the great spotted woodpeckers' roost, he fears for the wellbeing of the wrens, and finds his wife and neighbours keep disturbing the mistle thrush(n)

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore uncovers the role of religion in creating and maintaining the power of the city. In the first episode, he explores how every event in ancient Rome revolved around worship and that nothing took place without calling on the Pantheon, the temple to their gods. He also reveals the part played by sacredness and sacrifice in Roman culture(n)

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Guided by his Bradshaw's 1913 Handbook of Indian, Foreign and Colonial Travel, Michael Portillo embarks on a rail journey from Amritsar to Shimla. Along the way he helps to feed people at the world's largest free kitchen, and travels the railway routes used by millions of migrants during Partition. Beginning in the Sikh holy city, Michael is dazzled by the beauty of the Golden Temple and awed by the scale of its langar - the world's largest free kitchen. His route then takes him through the Punjab, and at Kalka, Michael glimpses the Himalayas for the first time(n)

programme.title: The Culture Show
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke explores the often overlooked history of the wartime art boom, meetingBlitz survivors, factory workers and Land Girls who became the subject of paintings, talks to contemporary artists about the challenges of creating work in conflict zones and considers how the images produced during the 'People's War' laid the groundwork for a modern understanding of what art should be(n)

programme.title: Victoria: A Royal Love Story
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce traces the monarch's love story by examining artefacts from a collection at the Queen's Gallery, Buckingham Palace. She looks at the paintings and sculptures she and Albert gave one another throughout their marriage, as well as meeting Prince Charles and visiting the couple's homes at Balmoral and on the Isle of Wight to understand more about their life together. Fiona also talks to the craftsmen restoring some of the objects today(n)

programme.title: Walt Disney
programme.desc: The first of two documentaries exploring the life, career and legacy of Walt Disney, making use of archive footage alongside interviews with his friends, colleagues and relatives to present a portrait of a ruthlessly driven animator and entrepreneur whose single-minded ambition allowed him to create a string of cartoon masterpieces that would define an entire industry. This opening instalment charts Disney's early days, from the creation of Mickey Mouse through to the triumph of Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs, his first full-length animated feature film(n)

programme.title: Walt Disney
programme.desc: Conclusion of the documentary exploring the life, career and legacy of Walt Disney, making use of archive footage alongside interviews with his friends, colleagues and relatives to present a portrait of a ruthlessly driven animator and entrepreneur whose single-minded ambition allowed him to create a string of cartoon masterpieces that would define an entire industry. The second episode explores Disney's later years, as he made films such as Cinderella and Mary Poppins, and realised his dream project - Disneyland(n)

programme.title: Metalworks!
programme.desc: In the first of three films exploring the importance of metals in British history, Dan Cruickshank charts the popularity of silver during the 18th and 19th centuries. He explains how it became regarded as a symbol of taste, wealth and status in Georgian and Victorian society, and visits locations around the UK to inspect some of the most notable examples of silverwork from the period(n)

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore uncovers the role of religion in creating and maintaining the power of the city. In the first episode, he explores how every event in ancient Rome revolved around worship and that nothing took place without calling on the Pantheon, the temple to their gods. He also reveals the part played by sacredness and sacrifice in Roman culture(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom's Year with the Birds
programme.desc: The wildlife expert films blue tits, blackbirds and swallows as spring arrives. He also realises the great spotted woodpeckers have abandoned their roosting site, but is delighted to discover a pair of Canada geese have nested on his pond and five goslings have hatched(n)

programme.title: New Forest: A Year in the Wild Wood
programme.desc: Peter Owen-Jones spends 12 months in the New Forest National Park in Hampshire, exploring its wildlife and history and meeting the people whose ancient way of life has helped shaped the land since Neolithic times(n)

programme.title: Tutankhamun: The Truth Uncovered
programme.desc: What killed Tutankhamun? Ever since the tomb of the boy king was discovered in 1922, he has become arguably the most famous pharaoh of all ancient Egypt. But his mysterious death at just 19 years of age has never been explained. Now, Dallas Campbell reveals new scientific research aimed at getting to the truth. A CT scan of the pharaoh's mummified body creates a full-size image of the real Tutankhamun, while DNA analysis uncovers a shocking secret about his family background - and the genetic trail leads to a revolutionary new theory about his sudden and unexpected death(n)

programme.title: The Search for Alfred the Great
programme.desc: Neil Oliver follows the work of the team of historians and scientists trying to discover the final resting place of the remains of 9th-century English king Alfred the Great, with the monarch's bones having been moved so many times over the years that many people concluded that they were lost for ever. Travelling from Winchester to Rome, Neil also tells the story of Alfred's life, revealing a man who forged a united language and identity and laid the foundations of the English nation(n)

programme.title: Sheila Hancock Brushes Up: The Art of Watercolours
programme.desc: The actress presents a history of watercolour painting, discovering a catalogue of little-known works and exploring how the medium's immediacy and freedom has given it an enduring appeal for both amateur and professional artists. She visits locations including the Alps, India and Venice to tell the stories behind paintings that were created there, as well as examining the importance of watercolours to artists in the days before photography(n)

programme.title: Jerusalem: The Making of a Holy City
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore charts the history of tension between Muslims, Christian and Jews in the city. He begins by focusing on the Mamluks' period in charge of Jerusalem following the Crusades, before exploring how life changed under the rule of the Islamic Ottoman empire. He also delves into the city's turbulent history during the 19th century, when it became prized by several European powers, and traces the origins of Jerusalem's present-day religious conflicts(n)

programme.title: British Art at War: Bomberg, Sickert and Nash
programme.desc: Presenter Andrew Graham-Dixon explores how three British artists - David Bomberg, Walter Sickert and Paul Nash - responded to the cataclysm of the First World War. He begins by focusing on the work of Paul Nash, who sketched the battlefields of Flanders near Ypres on 25th May 1917. He was so fixed on his work that he tripped and fell into a trench, breaking his ribs - an accident that went on to save his life and influence his later work(n)

programme.title: Tutankhamun: The Truth Uncovered
programme.desc: What killed Tutankhamun? Ever since the tomb of the boy king was discovered in 1922, he has become arguably the most famous pharaoh of all ancient Egypt. But his mysterious death at just 19 years of age has never been explained. Now, Dallas Campbell reveals new scientific research aimed at getting to the truth. A CT scan of the pharaoh's mummified body creates a full-size image of the real Tutankhamun, while DNA analysis uncovers a shocking secret about his family background - and the genetic trail leads to a revolutionary new theory about his sudden and unexpected death(n)

programme.title: New Forest: A Year in the Wild Wood
programme.desc: Peter Owen-Jones spends 12 months in the New Forest National Park in Hampshire, exploring its wildlife and history and meeting the people whose ancient way of life has helped shaped the land since Neolithic times(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom's Year with the Birds
programme.desc: As spring makes way for summer in Exmoor, wildlife expert Johnny Kingdom is relieved to discover a healthy brood of wren chicks and finds that some of the adults survived the harsh winter. He is also pleased with his footage of young swallows, and returns to one of his old hides in a bid to film the offspring of a pair of barn owls, as well as enlisting the help of an old friend to record the behaviour of woodpecker chicks(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the contrasting ways in which Mary, Queen of Scots and James VI forged political union with England and established Great Britain. While Mary aimed to seize the English throne from Elizabeth I, her son's method was perhaps even more radical and certainly had the longer-lasting effect of creating the Protestant nation that exists to this day(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez looks back at the discovery of three extraordinary pre-war treasures, and the explorer-archaeologists who came across them. In the first episode, she explores the surprise discovery in a Suffolk garden of the Sutton Hoo Hoard, an incredible Anglo-Saxon ship-burial, dating from the early 7th century AD(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: In the year 793, Europe changed for ever when the famous monastery at Lindisfarne on the Northumbrian coast was suddenly attacked and looted by seafaring Scandinavians. Alice Roberts examines how the story of the Vikings has been portrayed on TV since the 1960s. She investigates how the focus has shifted from viewing them as brutal barbarians to pioneering traders, able to integrate into multiple cultures. She also considers how their huge trading empire spread and their surprising legacy in the modern world(n)

programme.title: The Wonderful World of Blood - with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: Documentary in which Michael Mosley undergoes six experiments in order to take an in-depth look at blood, including injecting it with snake venom to demonstrate its ability to clot within seconds, and starving it of oxygen to see how it instantly responds. Michael also discovers how it tastes when he tries some black pudding made from his own blood(n)

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: When Lily is upset to realise Matt is going out with cool girl Greta Masters, Jas plans a make-over slumber party to cheer her up. Unfortunately it clashes with a rare comet sighting, which Martha and Ollie are desperate to experience. They have even been allowed to borrow the very precious school telescope for the evening. Ollie is smuggled in to Lily's house by Martha to watch secretly from the attic, with Martha planning to split her time between both events. After much chaos and confusion, and with the presence of Ollie in the house rumbled, a compromise is finally reached, where the girls all get to do both the make-over and watch the rare comet. At the end of the make-over session Martha and Jas are horrified to see Lily has transformed herself to look exactly like Greta in a bid to impress Matt. To spare Lily's feelings they pretend to like her new look, but are relieved the next day in school to see Lily returned to her normal, natural self. Lily has realised it's better to be(n)

Boris Johnson has pledged to boost school funding - but will it be enough, and will the extra money go to the schools that need it most?(n)

Cotswolds-based comedy, written by and starring Daisy May Cooper and Charlie Cooper(n)

Keanu tracks down Ben and tries to force him to withdraw his statement, but the pair struggle to contain their anger(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: As the town is engulfed by a storm, Jac faces a devastating confrontation with her old rival Faye Byrne and the man whom they both loved. Sacha helps a grieving family that is torn over the issue of organ donation to come to a difficult decision. Patsy Kensit reprises her role as Faye for the first time since 2010(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Holliday Grainger and Callum Turner star in a six-part crime thriller. Soldier Shaun Emery has his conviction for murder overturned because of flawed evidence, and returns home to Britain a free man. However, when damning CCTV footage of him comes to light, he ends up the subject of another investigation. Newly promoted detective Rachel Carey is assigned to the case, and quickly learns the truth is not as simple as it appears(n)

programme.title: Canny Cops
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the Durham Constabulary, who must keep the peace in a county that has been heavily affected by austerity and deprivation in recent years. Officer Terry takes on a new role in the remote village of Horden, where he pays a visit to a local woman whose house frequented by some of the area's most well-known criminals. Meanwhile, a masked man steals over £3,000 in an armed robbery at a bookmakers(n)

programme.title: Reggie Yates: Extreme Russia
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Russia, discovering what life is like for young people in what has been described as the toughest place in Europe to be gay. He visits St Petersburg to attend QueerFest, a 10-day arts and culture festival thrown in support of the LGBT community, and later hears from a young lesbian who was stabbed and left for dead by homophobes(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: The Welsh Italians
programme.desc: Merthyr-born Michela Chiappa presents the first of a two-part documentary in which she explores the modern Welsh-Italian community. As she visits cafes and fish-and-chip shops, she finds that while some traditions are the same, others have changed, and while food and family still unite the two cultures, things are less clear-cut when it comes to sport and which nation to support(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the four celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. Then, the celebrities are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge at London Zoo(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Sharon Osbourne investigates her family history. Recalling her less-than-idyllic childhood and her remote relationship with her mother, Sharon delves into her maternal family history and uncovers the difficult circumstances of her mother's and grandmother's lives. Later, Sharon discovers that she had a great grandmother who was born in the United States, but soon comes to realise that her great-great-grandparent's hope of living the American Dream did not go according to plan when they were faced with the harsh reality of work in a cotton mill(n)

programme.title: Abused by the Police
programme.desc: Following the on-going high profile case of teenager Anna Chambers, who alleges she was raped by two NYPD officers while under arrest in 2017, Ellie Flynn investigates cases of sexual assault and misconduct involving on-duty police officers and women held in their custody in America(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace goes behind the scenes with Britain's biggest food retailers to discover how technology and science are being used to bring consumers their food. In the first episode, Gregg examines the ways that supermarkets are fighting to be first with the latest food fashions, and discovers how online shopping is transforming the way people purchase their food and what they put in their trolleys. He joins Tesco's Head of Plant Innovation as he invents new vegan meals that he claims will taste like meat, and also finds out how Asda are using ingenious growing techniques to make their tomatoes sweeter than strawberries(n)

programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the four celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. Then, the celebrities are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge at London Zoo(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Sharon Osbourne investigates her family history. Recalling her less-than-idyllic childhood and her remote relationship with her mother, Sharon delves into her maternal family history and uncovers the difficult circumstances of her mother's and grandmother's lives. Later, Sharon discovers that she had a great grandmother who was born in the United States, but soon comes to realise that her great-great-grandparent's hope of living the American Dream did not go according to plan when they were faced with the harsh reality of work in a cotton mill(n)

programme.title: Abused by the Police
programme.desc: Following the on-going high profile case of teenager Anna Chambers, who alleges she was raped by two NYPD officers while under arrest in 2017, Ellie Flynn investigates cases of sexual assault and misconduct involving on-duty police officers and women held in their custody in America(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace goes behind the scenes with Britain's biggest food retailers to discover how technology and science are being used to bring consumers their food. In the first episode, Gregg examines the ways that supermarkets are fighting to be first with the latest food fashions, and discovers how online shopping is transforming the way people purchase their food and what they put in their trolleys. He joins Tesco's Head of Plant Innovation as he invents new vegan meals that he claims will taste like meat, and also finds out how Asda are using ingenious growing techniques to make their tomatoes sweeter than strawberries(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Whitney and Callum's wedding arrives, but with tensions high after Callum's revelation that he had a fling with Ben, Whitney struggles to put her fears aside. programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report on whether a crisis point in education funding has been reached, with cameras following staff and pupils at North Denes Primary in Great Yarmouth through a challenging summer term. To balance the books, the headteacher has to lose almost a fifth of her staff and cut services that help families in the wider community. Boris Johnson has pledged to boost school funding - but will it be enough, and will the extra money go to the schools that need it most?(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. The US Constitution is by contrast essentially a legal document. This has led Americans to address what should be political questions via the courts, rather than through politics. Recorded in front of an audience at George Washington University in Washington DC(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report on whether a crisis point in education funding has been reached, with cameras following staff and pupils at North Denes Primary in Great Yarmouth through a challenging summer term. To balance the books, the headteacher has to lose almost a fifth of her staff and cut services that help families in the wider community. Boris Johnson has pledged to boost school funding - but will it be enough, and will the extra money go to the schools that need it most?(n)

programme.title: Druthag na Hearadh (A Little Drop of Harris)
programme.desc: Seven years in the planning, two years in its construction, the Harris Distillery is in its first year of existence. This first of three episodes follows the lead up to and first working year at the Harris Distillery. After some delays in construction and the inevitable technical glitches, their Harris Gin goes straight into production. Hot on its heels is the distillery's primary function, and the team works up to distilling its first Hearach whisky. The distillery staff have all been recruited locally and are collectively known as the 'Tarbert Ten'. They share a commonality in having never been involved in the distillery business, but are keen to prove that they are more than able to create a historic happening on the Isle of Harris(n)

programme.title: Clann Pheter Roraidh
programme.desc: The story of the Campbell family from the Isle of Barra. Tony Kearney is a well-known Scottish actor and broadcaster, but his family's talents extend back generations. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tyrone suspects Hope of lying about the fire, and Geoff makes a fool out of Yasmeen. Elsewhere, Seb invites Mary to take Eileen's place at Number 11(n)

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: When 22-year-old Sian O'Callaghan goes missing after a Friday night out in Swindon, Detective Superintendent Stephen Fulcher knows that he and his team must move fast to have any chance of finding her alive. While Sian's family desperately wait for news, the police search for any clues as to where she could be. Fact-based crime drama, starring Martin Freeman, Imelda Staunton and Siobhan Finneran(n)

programme.title: Fighter Pilot: The Real Top Gun
programme.desc: In South Carolina, the Lightning Force pilots learn to drop bombs, including junior recruits Chris and Liam, who could find themselves ready for frontline duty by the end of the year. programme.title: Save Money: Good Health
programme.desc: Sian Williams takes a look at products aimed at fighting gum disease, while Ranj Singh answers viewers' questions on common concerns regarding children's health, from head lice to chicken pox. Plus, two members of the public try out different treatments for dandruff and the weekly guide to vitamin and mineral supplements continues with calcium(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh prepares a surprise for Rob and Margaret Isdale, a Grantham couple in their seventies who have fostered more than 150 children over the past 45 years. Alan draws inspiration from Frances Hodgson Burnett's novel The Secret Garden, creating ancient looking follies and winding paths, and even turning a normal garden shed into a mock-Tudor feature(n)

programme.title: Fighter Pilot: The Real Top Gun
programme.desc: The three students face the final aerial combat and mission tests to decide if they are ready to take to the skies in the new F35 Lightning fighter jet. The squadron they hope to join returns to Britain from training in South Carolina, and junior pilot Liam must master one of the most difficult manoeuvres he will ever have to face - mid air refuelling(n)

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Bedford Hills Correctional Facility in New York State to meet Sheila Davalloo, who has been convicted for murdering Anna Lisa Raymundo. However, in a bizarre twist to the story, Sheila has not yet started her 50-year sentence. First she must finish the 25 years she was given for the attempted murder of her husband. Sheila has remained silent about the killing for 14 years, so Piers tries to get her to finally open up, while also looking into why she wanted both of her victims out of the way(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Cole's former mentor from his CIA years Tom Barnes appears with some alarming news, as he and Murtaugh set about investigating the robbery of safe deposit boxes, whose suspects seem connected to his past. Meanwhile, Murtaugh encounters his wife's new client Louie Gutierrez. 'The Gute', as she is colloquially known, immediately proves to be a strong adversary, causing upset with every move she makes, prompting him to enlist Bailey's help to investigate her. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott, with Chandler Kinney(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: On the school trip, Lisa struggles to get close enough to Kate to ask her what has been going on, and watches in shock as Kate continues to play the role of the tragic mother of a missing daughter. The tension amidst the parents begins to rub off on the children, and a playful accident sees Fergus rushed into hospital. Lisa follows Kate and Fergus to hospital, determined to uncover the truth. Later, Roz is forced to tell Abby that Winston has moved out. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness offers one last group of men the chance to enter the 'love lift' and win the hearts of his 30 single ladies. Tonight's would-be Romeos are Jono, a musical theatre performer from Mansfield, City trader Andrew from London, Blackpool car salesman Ian, and Jon, an account executive from Milton Keynes. Plus, Paddy catches up with last week's couples on the Isle of Fernandos(n)

programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl (2003)

programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest (2006)
programme.desc: Wily rogue Captain Jack Sparrow finds an old debt coming back to haunt him. Years before, he sold his soul to the legendary Davy Jones, who has now risen from the depths of the ocean to collect what he is owed - with a giant sea monster at his command. Meanwhile, ruthless trader Lord Beckett is intent on seizing control of the Caribbean, and sets out to turn Jack and his allies against one another. programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End (2007)
programme.desc: The swashbuckling rogues join forces with old foe Barbossa to take on villainous Lord Beckett of the East India Trading Company, who plans to extinguish piracy forever. But first they have to journey to the ends of the Earth to free Captain Jack Sparrow from his hellish imprisonment in Davy Jones' Locker. When the group return from their rescue mission, they join forces with the nine Pirate Lords to fight for their freedom once and for all. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Johnny Depp, Geoffrey Rush, Orlando Bloom, Keira Knightley and Chow Yun-Fat, and featuring a cameo appearance by Keith Richards(n)

programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest (2006)
programme.desc: Wily rogue Captain Jack Sparrow finds an old debt coming back to haunt him. Years before, he sold his soul to the legendary Davy Jones, who has now risen from the depths of the ocean to collect what he is owed - with a giant sea monster at his command. Meanwhile, ruthless trader Lord Beckett is intent on seizing control of the Caribbean, and sets out to turn Jack and his allies against one another. Swashbuckling adventure sequel, starring Johnny Depp, Orlando Bloom, Keira Knightley, Jack Davenport and Bill Nighy(n)

programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End (2007)
programme.desc: The swashbuckling rogues join forces with old foe Barbossa to take on villainous Lord Beckett of the East India Trading Company, who plans to extinguish piracy forever. When the group return from their rescue mission, they join forces with the nine Pirate Lords to fight for their freedom once and for all. programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: The first episode of the whimsical rural drama based on HE Bates' novels. Tax collector Cedric Charlton visits the Larkin household intent on getting the head of the family - Pop - to correctly fill out his tax forms. In so doing he falls for attractive daughter Mariette. Starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A man who has not been heard of in 20 years and was presumed dead suddenly makes an appearance in Aidensfield - and seems not to have aged a day - prompting Merton to cross swords with an old adversary. programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When director Nick Cheyney is found beheaded by a guillotine on the set of his latest film, Barnaby and Jones are left with no shortage of suspects. The victim, it transpires, was on poor terms with several people in some way connected to the project, including the mother of his child, and an actor who blamed him for ruining his career. Guest starring Nicky Henson(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Preparations are under way for Rose's wedding, but family tensions on both sides threaten to derail proceedings. Edith's arrangement with Marigold is happily working out, but how long can she and Cora conceal the truth from Robert? Denker reveals an unexpected side to her personality, which causes trouble for more than one of the servants. With the war memorial due to be unveiled, Mrs Patmore is still upset, but Robert has something up his sleeve. Branson comes to a heart-wrenching decision, and the storm clouds that have been gathering over Anna and Bates finally burst(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Not for the first time, Jessica delves behind the scenes of the film industry, this time to investigate a stuntman's death in what should have been a routine car crash. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: PC Penhale is delighted by the arrival of his older brother Sam, but their reunion turns to misery when Martin delivers bad news to one of them. Louisa searches for a new home in the village and visits Edith for a check-up, where she reveals an embarrassing piece of information, and Al makes his situation with Pauline worse by trying to act on advice from Bert. Starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Al tries to raise some quick cash, and offers to sort out all Louisa's household and plumbing problems if she can pay him promptly. The teacher gets good news on the employment front, and Martin accidentally inflicts a minor wound on an elderly patient - who responds by walking out of the surgery and vowing never to return. Drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz, with Phyllida Law and Benjamin Whitrow(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: It's Murray and Bailey this week, as Rachel travels to Bristol with the DCI, who is advising on a rape and murder case that bears similarities to one she worked on 13 years previously involving the deaths of four women. Back at home, meanwhile, the detective discovers her brother Dom has been prostituting himself in her flat - and also learns the charges against her ex-boyfriend Nick are being dropped(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The duo look into the murder of an eight-year-old boy, coming up against a paedophile who cruelly deceives his victim's family. Dom's HIV test comes back negative, so he sets out to celebrate - only to endanger his own life and jeopardise Rachel's sergeant's exam in the process. Janet tells Andy she is having second thoughts about their brief liaison, but he finds her decision hard to accept. Suranne Jones and Lesley Sharp star, with Liam Boyle, Nicholas Gleaves and Joe Duttine(n)

Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons, Charlotte Bellamy and Tazmin Malleson(n)

programme.desc: Rigsby asks Philip for a native charm to help him seduce the delectable Ruth - but his dreams of a romantic liaison soon go up in smoke. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington(n)

Light-hearted drama based on the novels by HE Bates, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris, Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.desc: Lorraine tells her shocking story to Carol, who cannot believe she never knew what her husband was doing to her(n)

programme.desc: Don assures Denise he was not trying to buy her favours, Sally is pleased when Jonathan begins to settle down, and Jim finds Willmore in the bedroom with Liz(n)

programme.desc: Jim moves back into number 11, Willmore tells Liz the problem is her husband, not her, and Angie asks Steve to model a jacket she has made(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: It's the early autumn of 1924 and Rose's father-in-law Lord Sinderby has rented Brancaster Castle in Northumberland and invited the Crawleys to a grouse-shooting party. However, the Sinderbys' butler Stowell (guest star Alun Armstrong, New Tricks) has an axe to grind and a scandalous secret threatens to derail the holiday. Back at Downton, the servants hold the fort and unlikely later-life romances abound, while Bates takes drastic measures to clear Anna's name as she faces an uncertain future behind bars. The seasons change and another Christmas is enjoyed at the Abbey, where a heartbreaking farewell and a joyful reunion take place. Feature-length festive special, starring Hugh Bonneville, Maggie Smith, James Faulkner, Penny Downie, Joanne Froggatt and Brendan Coyle(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica visits a New York auction to buy a valuable manuscript and uncovers foul play among her rival bidders. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury as a crime writer with a mystery-solving sideline(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective receives a disturbing phone message from estranged brother Roy and heads to Harrogate to find him. Meanwhile at a murder scene, the team is introduced to new recruit Helen Morton, who quickly alienates her colleagues with her tactless style. The officer then makes matters worse when she finds evidence linking Banks to the victim's body and speculates he could be a suspect. Unaware everyone is searching for him, the DCI begins to delve into Roy's life and soon uncovers questionable business transactions and associates. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Caroline Catz (Doc Martin) and Andrea Lowe(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective visits his childhood home to break the news of Roy's death, and his parents plead with him to find out what happened. Defying Morton, Banks returns to the station in an attempt to lead the investigation into the murders of his brother and Jennifer Lewis, and both officers are convinced the key to cracking the cases lies with a woman being treated at a clinic(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. However, the murder of a rich industrialist neighbour forces him to leave his new lifestyle behind as he plunges into a sordid world of blackmail and envy, aided by the dogged Chief Inspector Japp. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris and Oliver Ford Davies(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Temperamental tenant Spooner, a professional wrestler, is confined to his room with a broken leg, and makes life hell for Alan and Philip by playing his radio full-blast at all hours - so naturally they challenge Rigsby to confront him. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ma can hardly contain her enthusiasm now that Mariette and Charley have announced their intention to marry. Unfortunately, Pop puts all his finances into bidding for a stately home, casting a shadow over her extravagant plans for the wedding. Rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nicholson delays his 21st-birthday celebrations to join Bellamy in pursuit of two armed and dangerous criminals, but takes one risk too many.
