programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Tucker acts like a lawyer in front of his father because he has forgotten to tell him he has dropped out of law school and works in TV. Meanwhile, Riley has a crush on a British guy, but Bonnie mistakenly thinks she has fallen for Danny. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau(n)

programme.title: Speechless
programme.desc: Maya's Halloween becomes truly terrifying when JJ attends a rave in the woods. Ray joins Dylan's Halloween heist and is determined to prove he's more than a do-gooder. Jimmy and Kenneth turn the DiMeo home into the neighbourhood's haunted house(n)

programme.title: Speechless
programme.desc: As JJ prepares for college, Maya seeks a new outlet for her parenting superpowers and sets her sights on a Ray. JJ gets involved in a high-stakes poker game and Dylan discovers what really happens after her bedtime(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Boyle go on an eight-day stakeout, which puts a strain on their friendship. Meanwhile, Jeffords uses Santiago and Gina for inspiration when he writes a book about his daughters, and Captain Holt's nephew visits Brooklyn and makes an instant impression on Diaz. Comedy, guest starring Kyra Sedgwick and Nick Cannon(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The team visits Boyle's beach house on a detectives-only getaway, but Peralta ruins the fun by inviting Captain Holt along. The host helps Diaz to send romantic texts to her new boyfriend Marcus, while Gina amuses herself by trying to get Santiago drunk. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Sheldon discover their favourite childhood TV science show host Professor Proton can be booked for private parties and decide to hire him to entertain them. Meanwhile, Raj asks Howard and Bernadette to look after his dog for the weekend, but the couple panic when the canine goes missing. Guest starring Bob Newhart(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys invite the girls to a Dungeons and Dragons night when their trip to Vegas falls through after an incident with airport security. However, following a few drinks, Sheldon and Amy's relationship takes an unexpected turn. Meanwhile, Raj goes on a date with Lucy, who tries to sneak out of the restaurant's bathroom window - but ends up getting stuck and calling for his help(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Zander and Ryan begin working on a graphic novel, but Lennox takes all the fun out of the project when she assumes some editorial responsibilities. Meanwhile, Mel attempts to persuade Joe to consider waiting before embarking on a physical relationship with his new partner. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel gets back in touch with a childhood friend, and as things progress romantically, Joe fears they could actually be relatives. Meanwhile, Lennox faces a moral dilemma when she discovers that her blog's new sponsor participates in unethical labour practices. Guest starring Trevor Donovan(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley gives Ben and Danny the time capsules they made at school, which causes everyone to reflect on what they have achieved in their lives so far. Meanwhile Bonnie is not happy when some unwanted attendees turn up at her dance class. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben is disappointed when he misses Emma's first steps so Bonnie, Tucker and Riley try to recreate the moment for him. However, the activity causes the group to contemplate how much their lives have changed since the child's arrival. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake is excited to work a case with the best detective in the NYPD, Dave Majors - until his colleague shares his intentions to ask Amy out on a date. Rosa's surprising fashion choice causes a stir, and Charles and Gina make an unexpected discovery. Comedy, with Andy Samberg(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Holt begins to worry he is being set up for a fall by one of his superiors, and Peralta is handed the chance to work on an important case. Meanwhile, Jeffords tries to make sure his twins get a place at their chosen school. Comedy, with Andy Samberg and Terry Crews(n)

programme.title: Speechless
programme.desc: Maya and Melanie take their new fashion line to an Abilities Expo, only to walk into a room full of familiar and unfriendly faces. Ray accompanies JJ on an overnight college visit. Dylan's test scores lead her to Mr Powers' office, and then on an inner journey(n)

programme.title: Speechless
programme.desc: JJ's love-match with Izzy becomes a cage match with Kenneth. Maya and Jimmy dismiss Valentine's Day as a holiday for gullible people until they spot a way to profit from it. Unable to find a date to a pre-paid romantic night out, Ray takes Dylan along instead. Minnie Driver, John Ross Bowie and Micah Fowler star(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard becomes concerned that Sheldon will overreact when his friend learns the truth about a past indiscretion, while Penny confronts Raj's old flame Lucy about the way she ended their relationship. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is dismayed when the group spends the Thanksgiving holiday at Mrs Wolowitz's house, but hits it off with Bernadette's father. Meanwhile, Penny realises she must deal with a mistake from her past when she and Leonard have an argument. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Peri encourages Cleo to embrace student life. Meanwhile, Liberty has a business idea, and Scott and Mitchell spend time together(n)

programme.title: Schooled
programme.desc: Lainey tries to convince Coach Mellor that arts are just as important as athletics by staging a school musical production. Principal Glascott and CB attempt to modernise the school's outdated health education programme for boys(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny is left in a difficult professional situation when a doctor (guest star Billy Bob Thornton) turns up at her door following her flirtatious sales technique, so the guys try to help her out. Meanwhile, Amy and Bernadette have a dispute over whether female scientists should flaunt their sexuality to try to get ahead at work. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Friends and family gather as Amy and Sheldon prepare to tie the knot - but it seems the happy couple's minds are elsewhere. Sheldon Cooper and Mayim Bialik star, with guest appearances from Laurie Metcalf, Kathy Bates, Mark Hamill, Jerry O'Connell and Teller (the quiet half of US magic duo Penn and Teller)(n)

programme.title: The 100
programme.desc: A small group decide to explore a mysterious new planet. Back on the Mothership, several members of Wonkru face the consequences of their decisions(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels across the United States in search of restaurants in dire need of renovation - and after analysing their situation, he has just 24 hours to bring them back from the brink of disaster. His first stop is the New York community of Congers, where he hopes to save family-run Italian restaurant Bella Gianna's. Gordon and his team work round the clock to clean the premises, remodel the interior and revamp the menu, while also teaching the staff to prepare new dishes and work together as an efficient unit. What will the customers think of the new-look restaurant?(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny runs into money troubles, so Sheldon offers to lend her some cash to help her pay the rent. However, when Leonard discovers his neighbour's ex-boyfriend Kurt is the cause of her financial woes, he gathers the gang for a confrontation. Starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny share an intimate moment as they recover from a psychological deconstruction by the physicist's mother, an expert in psychiatry and neural sciences. Guest starring Mamma Mia! actress Christine Baranski(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enters the paper crafts competition at the Royal Welsh Show. She produces an embroidered greetings card, and decoupage designer Jaina Minton teaches her how to make a paper sculpture of a seagull. To create jewellery, Phiona Richards lends a helping hand recycling damaged books and turning the print and pictures into rose rings(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits Vietnam, where a global surge in demand for pepper has transformed the lives of farmers across the country. She learns the difference between black and white varieties and the secrets of peppercorn quality control, and discovers the remarkable lengths to which producers go to guard their valuable crop from thieves. Jimmy Doherty meets a man whose job entails harvesting different strains of yeast from locations around the world and Matt Tebbutt looks into how manufacturers flavour crisps(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Nicole visits Angel to ask her about some wallpaper she found in her chateau. They talk business, and Angel gives Nicole a surprising tip. Tim and Margreeth prepare for Easter when their chateau will host 30 guests, but there are problems in the kitchen. Paul and Alison prepare a party to launch their new wedding terrace - but will the weather wash it out?(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Chocolate Week features a secret recipe challenge, and a Showpiece in which the teams have five hours to produce a magnificent sun, sea and sky-themed extravaganza with three types of chocolates. Their creations must demonstrate a mastery of chocolate, consummate technical ability, artistic flair - and a moving part. Presented by Liam Charles and Tom Allen(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Orang-utan sisters Emma and Subis are due to give birth at the same time, but after being in labour for more than 24 hours with no progress, Subis is rushed to the vet for a possible C-section. Meerkats Beagle and Huskie have moved to the zoo as the new breeding pair, but their new housemates, the aardvarks, are making them anxious. In an effort to make them more comfortable in their new home, the team decide to redesign the paddock to give the meerkats their own space(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B competition heads to France as four British expats compete, beginning with Lisanne Ashton, who runs the rural Les Chimeres in the south of the country. After searching for truffles in the surrounding area, guest Tony Horton's passion for the royal family causes him to clash with fellow competitor Shaun Baggs, whose mood is worsened by the discovery of bugs in his bed(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition continues at Le Manoir in the Dordogne, owned by royalist Tony Horton and his Armenian wife Shou Shou - who make a poor first impression by presenting guests with a timetable specifying when they are allowed to use the bathroom. A vinegar-tasting session also receives a tepid response, and some of the visitors are bemused by their hosts turning up for breakfast in costume(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wine-loving Shaun Baggs and his partner Patrick Weaver bring the contest to Le Clos Rabelais in central France, where they invite fellow competitors to take part in a traditional French dance class. Tension mounts as George and Linda become increasingly frustrated with Lisanne, and the bickering continues over dinner when Shaun is confronted about his previous negative feedback(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: George and Linda McGill welcome their guests to the Moulin de la Civiere near Rostrenen in Brittany, where entertainment comes in the shape of a tenpin-bowling outing, and the couple lay on a full English breakfast. However, the hosts receive an unexpected surprise at feedback time courtesy of Lisanne(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table(n)

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: George is joined by leading architects to help transform some of Britain's most unloved houses into desirable properties. The team meet Simon and Lisa, who recently bought a building in rural Gloucestershire in such a shoddy state that neighbours compared it to the house in Amityville Horror. George teams the couple up with creative architect Laura Clark, who attempts to turn this terrifying building into a warm and welcoming, modern home, whilst retaining some of its original character(n)

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan and Joan Bakewell host the painting challenge, as nine more contestants paint celebrity sitters Sophie Ellis-Bextor, Adrian Lester and Nitin Sawhney. The contestants have just four hours to complete their portrait, before the judges decide who to send through to the next round of the competition(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Elderly one-eyed South African porcupine Roxy is introduced to a new, younger male called Henning, in the hope that she will find companionship in her old age. Kifaru, the oldest eastern black rhino at Chester Zoo, meets younger female Malindi, with the expectation that they will produce offspring to increase the dwindling population, while the Caribbean flamingos are breeding again(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel suspects that an 18-month-old Australian Shepherd dog may be suffering from nerve compression, and an 11-month-old pug shows signs of neurological damage. It transpires that she has bone malformation that is squashing her spinal cord. Elsewhere, there is an emergency admission when a Jack Russell puppy called Bella is rushed in with a crushed pelvis after a freak accident(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge follows the fortunes of Brit Mandy and her American business partner Steve, who have decided to build a swimming pool to entice more paying guests to their stunning cliff-edge chateau in the Dordogne. Desperate to increase income at his chateau, Michael invites Angel Adoree to help out with an experiment to do afternoon tea for the locals(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Beautiful Railway
programme.desc: The Flying Scotsman returns to Edinburgh Waverley, where there is a special treat for one young lucky steam enthusiast, and engineers carry out essential checks on the Forth Bridge(n)

programme.title: Coastal Railways with Julie Walters
programme.desc: The actress travels from Newcastle to Edinburgh by high speed train. Along the way, she stops off at the coastal town of Alnmouth, discovers where the phrase Keep Calm and Carry On originated, visits the world's biggest gannet colony, and goes out to sea with a lobster breeder. When she reaches her destination, she discovers the connection between the railway and the famous Balmoral Hotel(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff of King's College Hospital reflect on the different ways in which people deal with physical and emotional pain as they treat a new batch of patients. Ahmed, 28, dislocated his shoulder during a game of American football and grits his teeth as a doctor slots it back into place. Meanwhile, 24-year-old Lee is living with Behcet's disease, a condition that affects his nervous system and causes pain so severe he passes out, and father-of-four Simon, 30, has been found unconscious at the bottom of a stairwell and it emerges he hasn't eaten for days following a relationship break-up(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: The harrowing events of one of the deadliest shootings in US history, when a gunman opened fire at Pulse nightclub in Orlando, Florida, killing 49 people and wounding 53 others(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel suspects that an 18-month-old Australian Shepherd dog may be suffering from nerve compression, and an 11-month-old pug shows signs of neurological damage. It transpires that she has bone malformation that is squashing her spinal cord. programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits Vietnam, where a global surge in demand for pepper has transformed the lives of farmers across the country. She learns the difference between black and white varieties and the secrets of peppercorn quality control, and discovers the remarkable lengths to which producers go to guard their valuable crop from thieves. Jimmy Doherty meets a man whose job entails harvesting different strains of yeast from locations around the world and Matt Tebbutt looks into how manufacturers flavour crisps(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits the Nidderdale agricultural show in the Yorkshire Dales, where she learns how to make her own pork pies and sausages. However, her success jeopardised by problems the evening before the event(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads to the Spanish city of Seville to look for the mysterious ingredient that gives marmalade its bitter tang, while Kate Quilton meets an entrepreneur who has developed an ecosystem that could revolutionise the future of fish farming. Matt Tebbutt hits the streets to see how billy goat meat goes down with the British public(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Margreeth concentrate on renovating the only caravan in their new park and the fire pit that will be its centrepiece. Nicole and Stephan make a drastic decision, and prepare for a party to celebrate the renovation of the main hall. Paul and Alison continue their search for the secret tunnel in their oubliette, and make the final preparations for a wedding, but bad weather throws both tasks into disarray(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The best six teams from both sets of heats are combined, facing new rivals and new challenges as they battle it out for a place in the quarter-final. For Holiday Week, the teams must create 24 classic brioches polonaises and 24 savoury Danish pastries in just four hours. For their next challenge, the teams take judges Cherish Finden and Benoit Blin on a journey with their holiday-themed Showpieces. Presented by Liam Charles and Tom Allen(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: African painted dogs K'mana and Ville were thrown together as a breeding couple after the dominant female died, but K'mana was never Ville's first choice as partner and still carries a limp from her battles with Ville's previous mate. Now she is pregnant with the zoo's first ever litter of this rare and endangered species. Meanwhile, keepers try to rekindle interest between Okapi pair Dicky and Stuma, and Atlas moths Dave and Simon only have five days of life after hatching, and their sole purpose is to mate(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owner Helen Marriott offers her guest a chance to 'step off the planet' and treats them to essential breathing lessons, a chance to get to know one another in the hot tub, and soul-soothing foot massages at the Fountains Court Holistic Health Hotel in the North Yorkshire resort of Scarborough(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants head to the Whittington & Cat, a pub in Hull that also offers B&B. Proprietor Sue Perkins treats her guests to an afternoon of ice hockey, but they are intrigued by the possible contents of her peculiarly titled breakfast menu(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Khurram Saba hosts the third night of the competition at his Saba Hotel in west London and finds his guests are expecting metropolitan chic, and certainly not fish and chips for dinner. Candida Leaver is extremely displeased when she experiences a sleepless night, while Sue Perkins and Gill take the opportunity to go out and have some fun(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Candida Leaver and Peter Starling, owners of the Bower Inn in Bridgwater, Somerset, play host in the last round of the week. The group activity is willow-weaving, and at dinner time the guests reveal what they think of their no-nonsense hosts(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table(n)

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: In the second episode, George Clarke is joined by leading architects to help transform a house belonging to Nick and Sonia, an appallingly designed flat spread over three dysfunctional car parking spaces. George charges award-winning architects Abigail Ashton and Andrew Porter with creating something beautiful from this monstrosity. But working with a budget of £75,000, can they transform this foul flat into a proper home with two floors? And what will the pair's neighbours make of the drastic changes?(n)

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan and Joan Bakewell host the painting contest. Nine contestants paint celebrity sitters Angela Griffin, Gina McKee and Jamael Westman, before the judges decide who to send through to the next round of the competition(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Stomp, a 13-year-old male okapi, is being encouraged to mate with younger female K'Tusha. But on his first attempt, he snaps his horn and causes K'Tusha a minor injury. Cameras at Chester Zoo also follow African painted dogs as they compete to be pack leader, and Raja the Bornean river turtle meets two prospective mates(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A French bulldog called Dave has a spinal deformity, and Noel discovers that not only does he have a genetic malformation of his sacral and lumbar vertebrae, but also excess bone in the pathways where the nerves travel to his legs. Meanwhile, a six-year-old terrier has lost a paw after being attacked by another dog, and the only options appear to be replacing the paw with a bionic implant or amputating the entire limb(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: As Jonathan and Michael prepare their chateau for the big wedding, they have a sewage problem that threatens to take the whole first floor out of action(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: A couple in Essex with five daughters plan to build a smart contemporary home in a woodland clearing, but the budgets and schedules soon get out of control(n)

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Mark Evans follows the progress of Ani Ridley and Charles Anderson who want to turn rusty old oil barge The Duchess 1910 into their rustic first home(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Sarah, 35, arrives at King's College Hospital concerned that a brain tumour she first had as a teenager has returned. Helen, 47, is worried about her baby son James, who was born with a hole in his heart and one kidney, and has been vomiting since the morning, while 28-year-old Wayne seeks treatment for knife wounds to his leg after he tried to stop someone stealing his girlfriend's phone(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, as guest captain Jason Manford and Joe Wilkinson take on guest captain Lee Mack and Fay Ripley. Alex Horne and the Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley takes care of the letters and numbers(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A French bulldog called Dave has a spinal deformity, and Noel discovers that not only does he have a genetic malformation of his sacral and lumbar vertebrae, but also excess bone in the pathways where the nerves travel to his legs. Meanwhile, a six-year-old terrier has lost a paw after being attacked by another dog, and the only options appear to be replacing the paw with a bionic implant or amputating the entire limb(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Sarah, 35, arrives at King's College Hospital concerned that a brain tumour she first had as a teenager has returned. Helen, 47, is worried about her baby son James, who was born with a hole in his heart and one kidney, and has been vomiting since the morning, while 28-year-old Wayne seeks treatment for knife wounds to his leg after he tried to stop someone stealing his girlfriend's phone(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads to the Spanish city of Seville to look for the mysterious ingredient that gives marmalade its bitter tang, while Kate Quilton meets an entrepreneur who has developed an ecosystem that could revolutionise the future of fish farming. Matt Tebbutt hits the streets to see how billy goat meat goes down with the British public(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits the Devon County Show, where she tries to impress the judges with scones and unconventional kumquat eclairs in the afternoon tea tent, which is famed for its competitive atmosphere(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty takes to the skies above Israel as he joins the battle to protect the bell pepper crop from the Mediterranean fruit fly, and Kate Quilton investigates premium-priced manuka honey, which is marketed for its anti-bacterial properties. Matt Tebbutt visits the UK's largest leek producer, where he's shocked by how much of the vegetable goes to waste, but amazed by a mobile factory on wheels that can process 60,000 leeks a day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie wants to plant a romantic rose garden for her singles weekend, but she cannot operate the digger. Dick and Angel need to rescue a children's trampoline from their moat(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the quarter-final of the competition, the teams are challenged to prepare 24 ice-creams and 24 doughnuts in just four hours, before creating a spectacular Showpiece incorporating confectionery, sugar work and chocolate. The lowest-ranking team must leave the competition, whilst the remaining four secure their place in the semi-final(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pregnant giraffe Orla goes into labour, but the keepers must watch from the sidelines due to the risks involved during the birth. Baby Bornean orang-utan Tombol tries his luck with the gibbons as he heads out and explores his new habitat. Elsewhere, northern bald ibis chick Professor X is proving too confident for his own good, and when it is time to release him into the adult habitat he gets into troubled waters and suffers a nasty head injury, so the vets have to work hard to help him pull through(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Spain, where Ben Eyre and Anna Chalu are first to host at Vista Del Torcal in the Andalusian Mountains. Despite being impressed with their rooms, some guests are less enthused by an afternoon of rock climbing, leaving Ben and Anna hoping a full English breakfast will save their bacon(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jed and Mandy Filmer welcome their rivals to the Hotel la Tartana on Spain's Costa Tropical, and hope to impress them with a cookery class - not realising one of their guests is a qualified chef. A revelation at dinner stuns the group, before the Filmers' attitude to breakfast service jeopardises their chances of victory(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Julia Grant hosts the third round of the competition in Spain at her Benidorm B&B The Queens Hotel. Her guests are not impressed with her standards of cleanliness, finding dirt, dust and hairs, and tensions rise at dinner over her 'straight-friendly' label. The owner improves the evening by giving everyone a drag makeover and persuading them to perform in a revue, but some of the feedback she receives leaves her enraged(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's final hosts in Spain are Alastair Smith and Martyn Watson, owners of the gay and naturist-friendly Almond Tree Villa in Murcia. The day's activity is olive picking, but the mood turns sour when Jed admits he gave Julia's hotel low marks, while a disaster in the bathroom and a sugar-rich continental breakfast reflect badly on Alastair and Martyn's feedback scores(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants of the Spanish special meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table(n)

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: George challenges innovative architect Greg Blee with transforming an impractical, upside down house with a mishmash of living spaces for Martyn and Sally. The building is so badly laid out that to get from the kitchen to the garden you must go through the master bedroom, but can Greg create a design that works on Martyn and Sally's budget of £65,000?(n)

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan and Joan Bakewell host the painting challenge. Nine contestants paint celebrity sitters Jodie Comer, Daniel Lismore and Nick Moran, before the judges decide who deserves the last spot in the semi-final(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Mr Parsons the chameleon meets a new partner, Daphne from Paris. Jaguars Napo and Goshi are being encouraged to mate, but Nap doesn't seem to be romantically inclined. Young giraffe Murchison faces the great outdoors for the first time, and another baby elephant is born, less than a month after the last new arrival(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats a seven-month-old fox red Labrador puppy with lameness, caused by a joint defect called osteochondritis dissecans. He explains that medicine can be given, but in the end the dog may end up requiring a complete ankle replacement or a joint fusion on both sides. Jo has travelled 400 miles to bring her beloved four-year-old springer spaniel to see Noel. The pooch's kneecap keeps slipping in and out of her knee joint, and despite several previous procedures, the problems persist. After an examination by Noel, Jo faces the further worry of osteoarthritis(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Jonathan and Michael prepare for a wedding day with 45 guests, and a swimming pool continues to cause problems for Mandy and Steve(n)

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Claire and Jamie leave Scotland, sailing to the West Indies on an urgent quest. When the crew looks for someone to blame after bad luck, rescue comes from an unlikely source(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Tree surgeon Kevin, 57, arrives at King's College Hospital in London with breathing difficulties, having fallen off a ladder onto a metal bar, while 11-year-old Archie has cut his lip in an accident at school and is worried about stitches. Elsewhere, a drink-related fall has left 53-year-old John with a head injury, and barman Ross, 31, needs treatment for a fractured eye socket following an alcohol-fuelled night out(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with captain Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson taking on guest captain Kevin Bridges and Jessica Knappett. John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank explore a disused shop, and visit a collector's garage where they find a motorcycle frame, a go-kart and some rare toys(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In an episode originally shown in 2011, Jeremy Clarkson celebrates the 50th anniversary of the Jaguar E-type by driving a modern-day interpretation of the classic sports car, and throws a typically low-key party involving live music, fighter planes and the Royal Marines. Richard Hammond is in South Africa, where he tests the Marauder, a bigger, more rugged alternative to the Humvee, and James May enlists the help of 2010 Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams and former Intercontinental Rally Challenge champion Kris Meeke to try out a Mini rally car. Plus, the Stig takes the BMW 1 Series M Coupe around the test track and Alice Cooper gets behind the wheel of the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: Police officers request a new police support unit or riot van, while a stolen car chase ends with an X5 wedged in a hedge. Boss Mick Sweetmore goes back to the floor with Merseyside's mental health team and discovers another side to modern-day policing - as well as the poor state of the vehicle they are currently using(n)

programme.title: Scrapyard Supercar
programme.desc: Engineer Ralph Hosier leads two teams of car buffs in their bid to build scrap cars. This time, they're up against pro driver Shane Lynch's Chevrolet Camaro in a plane-pulling contest. Building scrap able to get the 40-ton monster rolling will require innovation and a super-sized imagination.(n)

programme.title: Timber Kings
programme.desc: Joel builds a home he has never seen before using a crane he hopes he will never see again. Peter and Beat attempt to build the fastest troll on skis(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The trio travel to Italy to test high-performance hatchbacks. Jeremy Clarkson is in the Citroen DS3 Racing, Richard Hammond champions the Fiat 500C Abarth and James May makes the case for the Renaultsport Clio 200 Cup as they navigate their way round baffling Italian towns and take part in a scavenger hunt, before putting the cars through their paces on the F1 street circuit in Monaco. Also, petrolhead comedian Ross Noble is in the Reasonably Priced Car and the McLaren MP4-12C supercar comes under review(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond find second-hand bargains for the same price as the £6,995 Nissan Pixo. James May is in Nevada to test the toughness of the Range Rover Evoque, driving it through Death Valley before chauffeuring a megastar across Las Vegas. Plus, the McLaren MP4-12C is compared with its supercar rival the Ferrari 458 Italia, and four-time Formula One world champion Sebastian Vettel is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Celebrity guests Carol Vorderman, Larry Lamb, Jo Brand and Russell Howard join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the comedy panel show in which contestants try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon presents(n)

programme.title: QI

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Ultimate Worrier
programme.desc: Jon is joined by Tom Allen, Rosie Jones and Matt Forde to address worries to do with Holidays. Could they survive a foreign prison? Are drones getting out of hand?(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: One half of The Mighty Boosh and co-host of The Great British Bake Off Noel Fielding takes over the hosting reins of the long-running show as he comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo. The comedian, actor, artist, DJ and musician treats the audience to his own peculiar brand of surreal humour, before introducing routines by two up-and-coming stars of the comedy circuit, with south Londoner Dane Baptiste and Irishman Al Porter taking to the stage at the famous venue(n)

programme.title: Asian Provocateur
programme.desc: Comedian Romesh Ranganathan sets out on another international odyssey to meet members of his extended family. This time, he meet relatives who left Sri Lanka and settled in the USA, and by visiting them in Florida, Romesh hopes to get a taste of what his life could have been like if his parents had opted to migrate to the US instead of the UK. However, for this trip, his mother Shanthi is tagging along, as she fulfils her dream of visiting her relatives across the Atlantic. Marshalled by his cousin Pratheep, who has arranged a week of all-American entertainment, Romesh and his mother sample life stateside, but when Romesh ditches Shanthi to attend a beer-chugging Frat initiation, things begin to turn sour between the pair(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson celebrates the many practical experiments and re-creations that he and the team have conducted over 20 years to unlock the mysteries exposed by their digs. The programme revisits some memorable highlights from shows of yesteryear, from making individual objects like Stone Age axes, Roman pewter bowls, medieval pottery and King Arthur's sword, to building an entire Iron Age house and a Roman machine to lift water from a deep well(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: In a special 20th anniversary celebration for the programme, Tony Robinson relives the best bits from more than 200 episodes, taking in major highlights such as digging up the garden of Buckingham Palace to reveal a crashed Spitfire and discovering the first stone circle to be found in Britain for 150 years. There is a reminder of how the show grew from nervous beginnings in a Somerset field into attempting city-wide digs and promoting nationwide events in which thousands of people spent the weekend digging in their own backyards(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn continue to relive the golden age of steam and how the Victorian railways created modern Britain. They visit Beamish in County Durham, learning how the rail companies first realised there was money to be made in moving people rather than commodities. Then Peter and Alex live the life of navvies, the men who built the thousands of miles of tracks in all weather conditions, and Ruth finds out what impact the new railroads had on the cottage industries. She also learns about the role of the guard, and the trio learn what precautions were necessary for taking a long journey on a train that was yet to include the necessary facilities(n)

programme.title: Prince Philip: The Plot to Make a King
programme.desc: Secret History documentary telling the inside story of the dynastic tensions that were unleashed when Princess Elizabeth, the future queen, fell in love with Prince Philip. It was felt that Philip was rough and ill-mannered - and with the Second World War still fresh in most people's minds, the royal and political elite disliked his German roots. But following the death of King George VI in 1952, the establishment feared a dynastic coup, with the Queen torn between the two sides. The documentary examines the complexities and frustrations of the royal marriage, as the monarch struggled to reconcile the love of her husband with the suspicions of her family and her government(n)

programme.title: The World at War

programme.title: Time Team Specials
programme.desc: An episode made in 2005, in which Tony Robinson and the team commemorates the 60th anniversary of VE Day by embarking on a dig at a site in east London that was bombed in the Blitz. The programme illuminates the wealth of finds through the memories of former residents who are brought back to relive their experiences of the dark days of the 1940s(n)

programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the emperor penguin and the wood frog, both of which survive in conditions so cold they seem to defy the laws of nature. programme.title: Time Team Specials
programme.desc: Tony Robinson follows the renovation of the Grange, a house in Ramsgate, Kent, formerly owned by 19th-century architect Augustus Pugin, co-creator of the Houses of Parliament. Pugin's designs for the home became a blueprint for thousands of properties built after his death and in 20 years he succeeded in changing the face of England. With the help of experts such as Grand Designs' Kevin McCloud, the Landmark Trust aims to restore a piece of history(n)

programme.title: Time Team Specials
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team investigate finds at Durrington Walls, Wiltshire, and consider what they reveal about Stone Age life. Among the discoveries are a Neolithic road which leads to Stonehenge and is believed to be the route of traditional burial ceremonies. Also examined is an arrangement of 160 trees in perfect circles, which may have provided a setting for traditional feasts(n)

programme.desc: Debra is angry to discover Ray recorded a Super Bowl game over their wedding video, and forces him to renew his marriage vows as a way of making amends. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with Lucia and Matt, who were split over what to do with their four-bed detached home in Royal Wootton Bassett, Wiltshire. With four cats, two dogs and a yard full of hens, the house was at bursting point. Kirstie heads to the pair's new home in Llangammarch Wells, Powys, where huge changes have occurred, including the addition of a peacock to the household menagerie. Do Lucia and Matt feel they got the life they really wanted after all?(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: To celebrate 20 years of Grand Designs, Kevin McCloud reveals his top five properties from the show, revisiting the buildings and people who have most inspired him over the years. From concrete-vaulted glass-fronted pavilions and heroic restoration projects, to handcrafted hobbit houses made of hedgerow, Kevin reflects on what it takes to build a truly great Grand Designs project(n)

programme.title: Kathy Burke's All Woman
programme.desc: The actress discusses the nature of compromise in a relationship, talking to Caroline Flack, who reveals how she was once tempted to give up her career altogether for the sake of her boyfriend, and Skunk Anansie singer Skin, who talks about her different experiences in relationships with both men and women. Kathy also goes on a chaste date with a gigolo, who claims to offer women freedom from having to compromise during sex, and discusses the neurochemistry of love with an Oxford academic, who stresses that friendship is equally important(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Holiday Resort
programme.desc: Cameras monitor life at the all-inclusive Holiday World Resort in Malaga on Spain's Costa del Sol to take a warm-hearted look at what Britons get up in the sun and what it takes to keep them happy. Those featured include the Lloyd family from Northampton, including 12-year-old Danny whose singing and dancing proves a hit at the aquafit class. The Wades from Southend-on-Sea, Essex, are hoping to bond via the resort's all-inclusive entertainment - provided the children stop bickering, while James and Leanne from Preston battle the crowds during the morning stampede for the pool(n)

programme.title: The Secret Teacher
programme.desc: Darren Ryemill, the owner of an award-winning recruitment agency, goes undercover at the all-boys Forest School in Wokingham, Berkshire. He is shocked by the building's state of disrepair, with peeling paint and leaking toilets, and school head Ms Austin is forced to remind Darren that he cannot fix it all. He focuses his search for students in the school's Return to Learn initiative, where boys are sent as an alternative to exclusion. He meets 15-year-old Levi, who spends much of his time out of lessons. But a heart-to-heart with Darren reveals there is more to Levi's behaviour than first meets the eye(n)

programme.title: Britain's Extreme Weather: Superstorms & Heatwaves
programme.desc: Morland Sanders looks at climate change and its link to severe weather events in the UK, which has seem tornadoes, torrential rain, lightning storms, flash floods and a record-breaking temperature of 38.7C in recent months. This programme features archive footage, personal stories and striking visuals of Britain's recent heatwave. He examines whether the nation's ageing infrastructure can cope with this ever more brutal onslaught, and what effect it will have on agriculture and the cost of living(n)

programme.title: Call the Cops
programme.desc: A look at Devon and Cornwall Police's struggle to meet the needs of the communities they serve when reoffending is high, and there are cuts to social and mental health services. Force incident manager Adele faces difficult decisions in protecting the lives of the public as she deals with the most serious and life-threatening cases that come into the Devon and Cornwall police control room. She has to determine whether the use of firearms should be authorised to tackle a 15-year-old in possession of a sword, and if there is time to wait 30 minutes for a negotiator to talk a woman away from the edge of a cliff(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs

When a tip-off leads the detectives to a popular online game, they discover what happened to the victim's older sibling, who mysteriously disappeared. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay and Christopher Meloni(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An accusation that a teacher coerced a 15-year-old student into having sex with her is met with the counterclaim by the now-pregnant woman that she was raped. Novak obtains a warrant preventing her from securing an abortion and subsequent DNA tests confirm the boy as the child's father. Huang's suggestion that a personality disorder drew her to the youngster is undermined by incriminating evidence that casts new light on the case. Guest starring Melissa Joan Hart (Sabrina the Teenage Witch)(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A CIA agent's fiancee is raped and murdered by a professional hitman, suggesting an assassination, but the detectives suspect a man resembling the victim's partner could have invented the story to cover up an affair. Crime drama, guest starring Dylan Walsh (Nip/Tuck), with Christopher Meloni(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The body parts of a murdered high-school wrestling coach are found scattered around the city, and Hawkes discovers he may have been responsible for e-mailing sexually explicit pictures of underage boys. Later evidence suggests two of his athletes could have hacked into his account and sent the images, but they deny killing him. Meanwhile, Flack is accused of using excessive force to subdue a suspect who died in his custody(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A passing buzzard drops a human eye into Stella's coffee cup, prompting a race to find the rest of the body before it is devoured by other scavengers. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A female cop is found stabbed to death in a park alongside a Hispanic male with a near-fatal gunshot wound. programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis re-examine a case closed since the 1960s after a vehicle containing the remains of a murdered man is dredged up from the Hudson River. Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt(n)

programme.title: How to Stay Young
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary in which Angela Rippon and Dr Chris van Tulleken investigate the latest experiments and research that could help put the brakes on the ageing process. The first programme focuses on the physical aspects, revealing how much is down to how people live their lives and how people's lifestyle choices can help combat the effects of ageing. Chris finds out how public attitudes to the elderly can have a negative impact, spending a day as an 80-year-old with the help of Hollywood-style prosthetics, and Angela visits a study in Germany comparing the benefits of dancing with conventional gym exercises(n)
